meaning: n.[医](脑伤或疾病所致的)计算困难,计算障碍
examples: Someone who has trouble with mathematics may have dyscalculia.
The word dyscalculia means “counting badly” which is ironic since those who suffer from it need to count often.
Research has found that dyscalculia, a learning disability focused around number and math concepts, is as common as dyslexia.
Much of the research concerns learning deficits, such as dyslexia and its numerical analogue, dyscalculia.
These findings are being used to devise a new test for diagnosing dyscalculia at an early age.
